title: Epitaph On Mrs Margaret Paston, Of Burningham In Norfolk.
content:
So fair, so young, so innocent, so sweet,
    So ripe a judgment, and so rare a wit,
    Require at least an age in one to meet.
    In her they met; but long they could not stay,
    'Twas gold too fine to mix without allay.
    Heaven's image was in her so well express'd,
    Her very sight upbraided all the rest;
    Too justly ravish'd from an age like this,
    Now she is gone, the world is of a piece.
meaning:
This evocative piece by John Dryden, titled "Epitaph On Mrs Margaret Paston, Of Burningham In Norfolk.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
